requirements.label: Return bullet-only, 1-3 word, non-generic concepts

requirements.gap: Strength ordering is covered, but cross-axis diversity across structure, vibe, emotion, and craft is not.

requirements.gap: Selection is required, but the plan omits the ingredient-picking guidance copy that explains how users should steer results.

requirements.label: Return exactly five Explore Similar recommendations
requirements.evidence: 7.5 Section 9 - returns 5 recommendations with reasoning per selected concepts

requirements.label: Support full Alchemy loop with chaining

requirements.label: Clear downstream results when inputs change
requirements.evidence: 7.4 Step 5 lets users go back and rerun
requirements.gap: Backtracking exists, but the plan never says upstream changes automatically clear downstream concepts and recommendation results.

requirements.label: Generate shared multi-show concepts with larger option pool
requirements.evidence: 7.4 Step 2 always returns 8 concept chips

requirements.gap: The plan covers spoiler safety and quality checks, but not the full shared guardrail set across all AI surfaces.

requirements.label: Make AI warm, joyful, and light in critique

requirements.label: Structure Scoop as personal taste mini-review
requirements.evidence: 7.5 Section 5 and 8.2 Scoop prompt

requirements.label: Keep Ask brisk and dialogue-like by default
requirements.evidence: 8.2 Ask prompt and 8.3 Ask mode

requirements.label: Feed AI the right surface-specific context inputs

requirements.evidence: 8.5 Quality Bar

requirements.evidence: 7.6 Person Detail - profile header with photo, name, and bio
requirements.gap: The plan provides a single profile photo, not the gallery treatment called for in the PRD.

requirements.label: Include ratings, genres, and projects-by-year analytics
requirements.evidence: 7.6 analytics - number of shows in collection and average rating
requirements.gap: The analytics omit genre breakdowns and projects-by-year views, covering only a smaller subset of the required signals.

requirements.label: Group filmography by year
requirements.evidence: 7.6 filmography grouped by role in a poster grid
requirements.gap: The plan addresses filmography, but groups it by role instead of by year.

requirements.label: Open Show Detail from selected credit
requirements.evidence: 12. Phase 3 - Navigation between shows and people
requirements.gap: The plan mentions navigation broadly, but never states that selecting a person credit should open Show Detail directly.

requirements.label: Include font size and Search-on-launch settings
requirements.evidence: 7.7 Settings local settings and 12. Phase 5 settings page

requirements.label: Support username, model, and API-key settings safely
requirements.evidence: 7.7 Cloud settings and 8.1 Provider Abstraction

requirements.label: Export saved shows and My Data as zip
requirements.evidence: 6.6 Export API and 9.4 Data Export - JSON download
requirements.gap: The export design produces JSON, not the required zip backup that packages saved shows and My Data together.

requirements.label: Encode export dates using ISO-8601
requirements.evidence: none
requirements.gap: The plan never specifies ISO-8601 encoding for exported dates.

top_gaps.label: Emit `commentary` plus exact `showList` contract — Without the exact structured Ask contract, the mentioned-shows row can break or mis-map titles, collapsing one of the main AI-to-detail navigation loops
Overlay saved user data on every show appearance — If saved-user overlays do not consistently win everywhere a show appears, the core product promise of your version of the catalog becomes unreliable
Removing status deletes show and all My Data — Ambiguous removal semantics create ghost-record risk and make collection membership harder to reason about for users, tests, and sync logic
Re-add preserves My Data and refreshes public data — A vague re-add path risks either losing My Data after removal or restoring stale fields, which directly undermines trust in persistence
Export saved shows and My Data as zip — Exporting raw JSON instead of a zip backup misses the explicit portability contract stakeholders will expect for data ownership and recovery

narrative.overall_posture: This is a structurally sound plan with meaningful product-contract gaps. It would likely produce a working Next.js and Supabase application with the right major screens, but several exact behaviors that make the product trustworthy remain under-specified, especially around Ask output structure, collection lifecycle edge cases, and export fidelity.
narrative.strength_clusters: The plan is strongest in Benchmark Runtime & Isolation, Show Detail & Relationship UX, and AI Voice, Persona & Quality. Those areas have concrete architecture, schema, routing, and phased build tasks, so a team could start implementation with relatively little ambiguity about the runtime, persistence model, or the major show-detail and AI voice surfaces.
narrative.weakness_clusters: The gaps are not random. They cluster in Ask Chat, Concepts, Explore Similar & Alchemy, and Collection Data & Persistence, where the PRD depends on exact behavioral contracts rather than broad feature existence. Person Detail and Settings & Export are also weak, but in a different way: they are present in the plan as shells, yet still off-spec on important specifics such as gallery depth, analytics shape, backup packaging, and export formatting.
narrative.risk_assessment: If this plan were executed as-is, the first thing a stakeholder or QA reviewer would notice is a product that looks broadly complete but breaks key invisible contracts. Ask could render recommendations without a reliable machine-readable mention payload, saved-state overlays could drift across browse surfaces, removed and re-added shows could behave inconsistently, and export would land in the wrong format. Those are not cosmetic misses; they affect trust, recoverability, and the app's taste-aware discovery loop.
narrative.remediation_guidance: The plan needs another pass focused on precise acceptance criteria, not more broad architecture. Tighten the data-lifecycle rules for removal, re-add, Scoop persistence, sync, and upgrades; specify the exact Ask and concept-response contracts including retries and domain redirects; define the navigation-shell and Search behavior more explicitly; and expand the Person Detail and export sections so they match the PRD's concrete UX and data-format requirements rather than placeholder feature shells.
